title: Living Lab Dashboards
text:
The Living Lab Dashboards project develops interactive web-based tools for visualizing and exploring data from living lab experiments conducted across our research network.
These dashboards integrate diverse data streams including environmental sensors, field observations, and remote sensing data to provide real-time insights into landscape dynamics and experimental outcomes.
The platform supports collaborative analysis and data-driven decision making for adaptive management of living lab sites.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text: Diese Website ist eine statische Website, die über GitHub Pages bereitgestellt wird. Wir erheben nicht aktiv personenbezogene Daten. Allerdings kann GitHub bei einem Besuch personenbezogene Daten verarbeiten (z. B. Protokolldaten einschließlich IP-Adressen), um rechtliche Verpflichtungen zu erfüllen sowie die Sicherheit und Integrität der Website und des Dienstes zu gewährleisten. Details finden Sie in der GitHub-Datenschutzerklärung.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text: Diese Website ist eine statische Website, die über GitHub Pages bereitgestellt wird. Wir erheben nicht aktiv personenbezogene Daten. Allerdings kann GitHub bei einem Besuch personenbezogene Daten verarbeiten (z. B. title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
1. Zweck dieser Datenschutzerklärung
Die Arbeitsgruppe „Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs (DML)“ am Leibniz-Zentrum für Agrarlandschaftsforschung (ZALF) legt großen Wert auf den Schutz Ihrer personenbezogenen Daten. Diese Datenschutzerklärung informiert darüber, welche personenbezogenen Daten beim Besuch dieser Website verarbeitet werden können, zu welchen Zwecken dies geschieht und welche Rechte Ihnen zustehen.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
2. Erhebung und Verarbeitung von Daten
Da es sich um eine statische Website auf GitHub Pages handelt, verarbeiten wir selbst keine personenbezogenen Daten aktiv. Im Rahmen des Betriebs können jedoch durch den Hosting-Anbieter folgende Daten verarbeitet werden:

Protokolldaten: Informationen wie IP-Adresse, Browsertyp, Betriebssystem, Referrer-URL, aufgerufene Seiten und Zeitstempel können von GitHub Pages protokolliert werden.
Cookies: Diese Website setzt selbst keine Tracking-Cookies. GitHub kann Cookies einsetzen, um seine Dienste bereitzustellen und abzusichern.

Weitere Informationen finden Sie in der GitHub-Datenschutzerklärung.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
3. Zweck der Verarbeitung
Wir verwenden personenbezogene Daten nicht für Analyse-, Marketing- oder Profiling-Zwecke. Eine etwaige Verarbeitung durch GitHub (z. B. Server-Logs) dient dem Betrieb und der Sicherheit der Hosting-Plattform.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
4. Weitergabe von Daten
Wir verkaufen oder vermieten keine personenbezogenen Daten und geben sie nicht an Dritte weiter. GitHub kann Daten als Hosting-Anbieter gemäß seiner Datenschutzerklärung verarbeiten.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
5. Rechte der betroffenen Personen (DSGVO)
Nach der DSGVO haben Sie insbesondere folgende Rechte:

Auskunft (Art. 15)
Berichtigung (Art. 16)
Löschung (Art. 17)
Einschränkung der Verarbeitung (Art. 18)
Widerspruch (Art. 21)
Datenübertragbarkeit (Art. 20)

Zudem haben Sie das Recht, sich bei einer Aufsichtsbehörde zu beschweren (Art. 77).

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
6. Speicherdauer
Wir speichern über diese Website keine personenbezogenen Daten. Etwaige Protokolldaten werden durch GitHub gemäß deren Aufbewahrungsrichtlinien verarbeitet.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
7. Dienste Dritter und externe Links
Diese Website kann Links zu externen Websites enthalten. Für deren Datenschutzpraktiken sind wir nicht verantwortlich. Bitte beachten Sie die jeweiligen Datenschutzhinweise.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
8. Sicherheitsmaßnahmen
Diese Website betreibt keine eigene Nutzerdatenbank. GitHub Pages stellt Betriebs- und Plattform-Sicherheit bereit. Weitere Informationen finden Sie in GitHub’s Security overview.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
9. Aktualisierungen
Wir können diese Datenschutzerklärung anpassen, um rechtliche Anforderungen oder Änderungen der Website abzubilden. Die jeweils aktuelle Version wird auf dieser Seite veröffentlicht.

title: Datenschutzerklärung
text:
10. Kontakt
Bei Fragen zur Datenschutzerklärung kontaktieren Sie uns bitte:

Leibniz-Zentrum für Agrarlandschaftsforschung (ZALF) e.V.
Arbeitsgruppe „Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs (DML)”
E-mail: benjaminsamuel.black@zalf.de

title: DaMIfLL at the FAIRagro Community Summit
text:
In March the Working group attended the FAIRagro community summit, joining researchers and data infrastructure specialists to discuss FAIR data principles in the agricultural and landscape sciences.
The event provided valuable opportunities to exchange ideas, identify common challenges, and explore potential collaborations between DaMIfLL and FAIRagro initiatives.

title: Daten- und Modellierungsinfrastruktur für Living Labs
text:
Diskutieren Sie Ihr Projekt mit uns
Willkommen auf der Website der Arbeitsgruppe Daten- und Modellierungsinfrastruktur für Living Labs (DMI). Wir sind eine Service-Arbeitsgruppe innerhalb des Innovationszentrums für Agrarsystemtransformation (IAT) am Leibniz-Zentrum für Agrarlandschaftsforschung (ZALF). Unsere Mission ist es, Forschende und Partner innerhalb des IAT in allen Belangen rund um Daten und Modellierung zu unterstützen. Die verschiedenen Möglichkeiten, wie wir dies tun, haben wir in der Grafik unten dargestellt:

title: Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs
text:
IAT Service Working Group


Discuss your project with us
Welcome to the website of the Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs (DMI) Working Group. We are a service working group within the Innovation Center for Agricultural System Transformation (IAT) as part of the Liebniz Center for Agricultural Landscape Research (ZALF). Our mission is to support researchers and partners within the IAT with all things related to data and modelling. We have represented the different ways that we do this in the graphic below:

title: DMI Presents at IAT Kickoff Meeting
text: In March 2026 the Innovation Center for Agricultural System Transformation (IAT) was officially launched, with two days of events at the Futurium in Berlin as well as the ZALF campus in Müncheberg. The focus of the 2nd day was on connecting researchers working on similar topics within the IAT from both ZALF and the partner institutions. We were fortunate to have an opportunity to present our concept for how the Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs group (DMI) will provide a three pillar approach of services to support IAT researchers and living labs in their data and modelling needs. The slides we presented are available below, and we are looking forward to engaging with the IAT and broader ZALF community in the coming months to produce some real outputs from collaborations to serve as a showcase for the services we can provide.

title: Upcoming Workshop and Hackathon at iEMSs 2026
text:
We are excited to announce that the DMI group will host both a workshop and a hackathon at the International Environmental Modelling and Software Society (iEMSs) 2026 conference in Dublin over the 12-16th July.
The workshop “One Publishing Tool to Rule Them All: Quarto for Documentation, Presentations, Manuscripts and Outreach”, which is being prepared in collaboration with researchers from ETH Zurich will explore Quarto as a unified solution for academic communication. Quarto can generate multiple output formats — PDF, HTML, LaTeX, and DOCX — from a single source document while supporting executable code in Python, R, Julia, and Observable. Through short presentations and guided exercises, participants will learn to produce documentation, reproducible journal articles, presentations, outreach websites, and interactive dashboards — all from one tool.
The Hackathon, prepared in collaboration with the Early Career Researcher Team within the Open Modelling Foundation will have participants try to ‘hack’ existing models to improve their FAIRness with a special focus on re-useability.
We will make the content from both the workshop and hackathon available online after the conference, so stay tuned for more updates!

title: Privacy policy
text: This website is a static site hosted on GitHub Pages. We do not actively collect personal data. However, GitHub may collect personal data from visitors (including logs of visitor IP addresses) to comply with legal obligations and to maintain the security and integrity of the website and service. See the GitHub Privacy Statement for details.

title: Privacy policy
text: This website is a static site hosted on GitHub Pages. We do not actively collect personal data. However, GitHub may collect personal data from visitors (including logs of visitor IP addresses) to comply with legal obligations and to maintain the security and integrity of the website and service. See the GitHub Privacy Statement for details.

title: Privacy policy
text:
1. Purpose of this privacy policy
The Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs (DML) Working Group at the Leibniz Centre for Agricultural Landscape Research (ZALF) is committed to protecting your personal data. This privacy policy explains what personal data may be processed when you visit this website, how it is used, and what rights you have.

title: Privacy policy
text:
2. Collection of data
Since this website is a static site hosted on GitHub Pages, we do not actively collect personal data. However, the following data may be processed by our hosting provider as part of standard operation:

Log data: Information such as IP address, browser type, operating system, referring URL, pages visited, and timestamps may be logged by GitHub Pages.
Cookies: This site does not set its own tracking cookies. GitHub may use cookies as part of providing and securing their services.

Please refer to the GitHub Privacy Statement for details on their handling of log data and cookies.

title: Privacy policy
text:
3. Use of data
We do not use personal data for analytics, marketing, or profiling. Any data processed by GitHub (e.g., server logs) is used to operate and secure the hosting platform.

title: Privacy policy
text:
4. Sharing your data
We do not sell, lease, or share personal data. GitHub, as the hosting provider, may process data according to their privacy policy.

title: Privacy policy
text:
5. Data subject rights (GDPR)
Under the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

Right to access (Art. 15)
Right to rectification (Art. 16)
Right to erasure (Art. 17)
Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18)
Right to object (Art. 21)
Right to data portability (Art. 20)

You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (Art. 77).

title: Privacy policy
text:
6. Data retention
We do not retain personal data through this website. Any log data processed by GitHub is retained according to GitHub’s policies.

title: Privacy policy
text:
7. Third-party services and links
This site may link to third-party websites. We are not responsible for their privacy practices. Please review the privacy policies of those websites.

title: Privacy policy
text:
8. Security measures
This static website does not operate its own user database. GitHub Pages provides operational and platform security. See GitHub’s Security overview for more details.

title: Privacy policy
text:
9. Updates to this policy
We may update this privacy policy to reflect changes in legal requirements or website functionality. Updates will be published on this page.

title: Privacy policy
text:
10. Contact
If you have questions about this privacy policy, please contact:

Leibniz Centre for Agricultural Landscape Research (ZALF) e.V.
Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs (DML) Working Group
E-mail: benjaminsamuel.black@zalf.de

title: MonksHillLabs Initiative
text:
MonksHillLabs is a collaborative research platform that provides shared computational infrastructure, data repositories, and development environments for landscape and land use modelling projects.
The initiative brings together researchers, data scientists, and software developers to co-create tools, standards, and workflows that advance data interoperability and reproducible research in landscape science.
Current activities include developing standardized data formats, building modular modelling components, and hosting hackathons and training workshops.

Check out the Open Science Awesome List

Quarto

Beautiful slides with RevealJS and Quarto

title: Resources and tools
text:
Tools and Software

EU alternatives for software - A directory of software alternatives to popular proprietary software, with a focus on European users and organizations.
gettingstuffdoneai - Directory of Generative AI Tools and Models, categorized by use case and functionality.
Inkscape - Open source vector graphics editor, alternative to Adobe products, useful for creating and editing figures for publications and presentations. Available via the ZALF baramundi client.

title: Resources and tools
text:
Training

Digital Research Academy - a trainer network focused on Open Science, Data Literacy, and Research Software Engineering (DRA’s Zenodo collection).
BERD academy
BERD reproducible research course
NFDI4Earth - Educational Portal
Assured Training -Ensures the safe, legal, and ethical use of sensitive data through targeted training and certification.
KI-campus - Numerous free courses on AI and related topics, including data science, machine learning, and ethical considerations in AI.
Liebniz Institute for the Social Sciences (GESIS)
